MOTU UltraLite-mk3

Section-based notes for routing, CueMix, converters, monitoring, and hardware setup.

  • Overview
  • Hybrid FireWire/USB2
  • Mic/Guitar Inputs
  • Comprehensive I/O
  • Mixing and effects
  • CueMix FX software
  • Classic reverb
  • Modeled analog EQ
  • Vintage compression
  • Tuner & analysis tools
  • Clock and sync
  • MIDI
  • Expansion & drivers
  • Feature Summary

Monitoring & Headphones

Main Output → HS7 Monitors

Assign CueMix FX Mix 1 to the Main Outputs (channels 1/2) and connect those to the Yamaha HS7 monitors with balanced TRS cables. The Mix 1 main output represents the studio reference monitoring environment — keep it flat and accurate.

Headphone Monitoring

The front-panel headphone output has its own dedicated volume knob, independent from the main output level. Assign a separate CueMix FX mix bus (e.g. Mix 2) to the headphone output for a completely different balance than what the monitors hear.

Zero-Latency Vocal Monitoring Chain

This is the optimal recording chain for tracking vocals through the SM58:

  1. SM58 → MOTU input 1 (XLR, mic preamp, +48V OFF).
  2. In CueMix FX, create a direct send from Input 1 → Mix 2 (headphone bus). No computer involved in this path.
  3. Add Classic Reverb send in Mix 2 for vocal comfort monitoring (keep the reverb only in the headphone mix, not Mix 1).
  4. In Ableton, set the vocal Audio Track Monitor to Off (prevents software-path doubling with the CueMix direct monitor).
  5. Vocalist hears their own voice with zero added latency through headphones connected to the front-panel PHONES jack.

Multiple Cue Mix Management

  • Mix 1 (Main/Studio monitors): flat, accurate, everything at session balance. What goes to the HS7s.
  • Mix 2 (Headphone cue): vocalist's headphone mix. SM58 input +6 dB relative to the backing track, reverb send active, everything else at normal level.

Both mixes can be configured and saved simultaneously in CueMix FX — no need to reconfigure when switching between studio listening and recorded tracking.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
